insight.headline: Storefront API Update
insight.what_changed: The Storefront API now supports the @inContext directive with an optional channelId argument, allowing developers to apply a specific sales channel's context to an entire query. This change is available in Storefront API version 2026-10. The @inContext directive enables developers to fetch data specific to a particular sales channel, such as online store or point of sale.
insight.developer_impact: Developers using the Storefront API should update their queries to utilize the @inContext directive with the channelId argument to take advantage of this new feature. This change enables more flexibility in querying data specific to different sales channels. Developers should review their existing queries and update them to include the channelId argument where necessary. Additionally, developers should ensure they are using Storefront API version 2026-10 or later to access this feature.
insight.merchant_impact: Merchants will benefit from more accurate and sales-channel-specific data being fetched by apps using the Storefront API, potentially leading to better app functionality and user experience.
insight.why_it_matters: This update matters because it provides developers with more control over the data they fetch from the Storefront API, allowing for more tailored and sales-channel-specific experiences. It also demonstrates Shopify's ongoing efforts to enhance the Storefront API and provide more features for developers.

also_worth_knowing.text: Metafield triggers and additional topics are now available for Events
Liquid templates can now compose pages with blocks and partials
Full-stack capabilities to power app analytics

updates.title: Metafield triggers and additional topics are now available for Events
updates.summary: Metafield triggers and additional topics are now available for Events, allowing apps to subscribe to events for various objects and metafield changes.

updates.title: Liquid templates can now compose pages with blocks and partials
updates.summary: Liquid templates can now compose pages with blocks and partials, simplifying theme page building.

updates.title: Full-stack capabilities to power app analytics
updates.summary: Full-stack capabilities are now available to power app analytics, simplifying the process of building merchant-facing analytics.

updates.title: Physical inventory feature preview
updates.summary: The physical inventory feature preview is available, providing early access to new inventory APIs in the unstable version of the GraphQL Admin API.
CLI version 4.5.2 fixes a warning when running theme commands in valid theme directories and bumps Shopify/theme-tools packages.

updates.title: Card deposit endpoint now requires mTLS certificate
updates.summary: The card deposit endpoint now requires a Shopify-issued mTLS client certificate, affecting apps that store cardholder data.

updates.title: @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.2
updates.summary: Hydrogen CLI version 13.0.2 adds non-interactive shop and storefront creation flags and allows bypassing confirmation prompts in hydrogen upgrade.

updates.title: Updated App Store requirements: 4.1.2 Use a unique name for your app
updates.summary: Updated App Store requirements include using a unique name for your app to reduce merchant confusion.

updates.title: Storefront API `@inContext` supports `channelId`
updates.summary: The Storefront API @inContext directive now supports channelId, allowing for specific sales channel context to be applied to entire queries.

updates.title: `productId`, `title`,`variantSku`, and `variantTitle` fields added to `ExchangeLineItem`
updates.summary: The ExchangeLineItem type now includes productId, title, variantSku, and variantTitle fields.

updates.title: POS UI Extensions 2026-07 adds discount allocations to bundle components
updates.summary: POS UI Extensions 2026-07 adds discount allocations to bundle components, allowing apps to access component-level discount allocations.

recap.headline: Enhanced event triggers, Liquid templates, and inventory APIs

themes.title: Event Triggers and Webhooks
themes.detail: Metafield triggers and additional topics are now available for Events, allowing apps to subscribe to events for various resources such as Order, Collection, and InventoryItem. This update enables more robust webhook integrations.

themes.title: Liquid Templates and Theme Development
themes.detail: The Liquid July ’26 developer preview introduces a simpler way to build theme pages using blocks and partials, making it easier to compose pages directly in Liquid templates. Additionally, full-stack capabilities are now available to power app analytics.

themes.title: Inventory APIs and Physical Inventory Feature
themes.detail: The physical inventory feature preview provides early access to new inventory APIs in the unstable version of the GraphQL Admin API, enabling developers to build and test against these APIs on development stores.

actions.text: Update webhook subscriptions to utilize new event triggers
Explore the Liquid July ’26 developer preview and update theme templates
Enable physical inventory feature preview on a development store
